text:
Snow develops early, then mixes with and changes to rain from south to north by midday; stays mostly snow with only limited mixing in the Poconos. 1-3" along I-78 corridor, 1" or less farther south. Up to 3-6" Poconos and far NW NJ. .

Gradual clearing, brisk, and chilly; watch for slick spots.

Updated: December 2, 2025 @ 11:57 am

Bulletin: ...WINTER WEATHER ADVISORY NOW IN EFFECT UNTIL NOON EST TODAY...

* WHAT...Mixed precipitation. Additional snow accumulations up to
one inch and ice accumulations around a light glaze.

* WHERE...In New Jersey, Somerset and Hunterdon Counties. In
Pennsylvania, Upper Bucks and Western Montgomery Counties.

Bulletin: ...FIRST WIDESPREAD SNOW ACCUMULATION OF THE SEASON...

.The first widespread snow accumulation of the season will impact
central Pennsylvania from late tonight into early Tuesday afternoon.
Hazardous travel is expected during the Tuesday morning commute with
a plowable snowfall expected. Mixed precipitation is likely near the
Mason-Dixon line, where a glaze of ice is possible. Localized
snowfall totals over 6 inches are possible at higher elevations to
the east of US-15 and north of I-81/I-78 into the southern Poconos.

...WINTER WEATHER ADVISORY REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 1 PM EST THIS
AFTERNOON...

* WHAT...Snow expected. Total snow accumulations between 4 and 6
inches.

* WHERE...A portion of central Pennsylvania.

* WHEN...Until 1 PM EST this afternoon.

* IMPACTS...Plan on slippery road conditions. The hazardous
conditions will impact the morning commute.

PRECAUTIONARY/PREPAREDNESS ACTIONS...

Slow down and use caution while traveling. The latest road
conditions for the state you are calling from can be obtained by
calling 5 1 1.

Reporter







BETHLEHEM, Pa - Fans of a former Bethlehem Catholic and Lehigh University standout are gathered at an area restaurant Thursday morning as they cheer for him at the Olympic Games in Paris.

They watched as Darian Cruz was able to get a pin against Egypt during round one and advance to the quarter-finals. In his second match, Cruz fell to Japan.

Cruz is representing his grandparents’ native Puerto Rico when competing in the men’s freestyle 57 kg wrestling division.

WATCH: 69 News was live as fans watched Cruz advance to the quarter-finals.

Cruz won the first match at 57 kg by pinning Egypt’s Gamal Mohamed in 2:30, 4-1.

In the quarters, Cruz fell 12-2 to top seed at 57 kg, Rei Higuchi.

Higuchi of Japan advanced the finals in a Thursday afternoon match. He goes to the finals on Friday.

With Higuchi's win, Cruz gets pulled back in. He has a shot at the bronze medal on Friday.

Cruz will compete against Aman Sehrawat of India., who lost to Higuchi on Thursday.

69 News reporter Priscilla Liguori caught up with wrestler Darian Cruz ahead of his trip to the Olympics in Paris.

Cruz won two PIAA Class 2A individual wrestling titles at Bethlehem Catholic in 2012 and 2013 and a NCAA Division I national title at Lehigh University in 2017.

Anthony Spagnola, owner of Sotto Santi Pizzeria Pub in south Bethlehem, organized the Olympics watch party for Cruz.

The watch party included a $10 all-you-can-eat buffet, featuring breakfast selections such as quiches, breakfast pizza, assorted muffins, fresh fruit, coffee and orange juice.

Stick with 69 News for updates.

Former Bethlehem Catholic and Lehigh University standout, Darian Cruz is looking to add to his long list of accolades in Paris. Cruz is set to compete for team Puerto Rico on the mat during the Summer Games.

title: Paris Olympics: Lehigh Valley wrestler Darian Cruz falls to Japan in quarter-finals, will compete for bronze medal | Lehigh Valley Regional News | wfmz.com
author: Blakely McHugh
description: Fans watched as Darian Cruz was able to get a pin during round one. Cruz fell to Japan in the quarters.
keywords: darian cruz, lehigh university, anthony spagnola, sotto santi pizzeria pub
